items.caption: A bar chart titled "number of unique chemicals detected by chemical group per basin (excludes new methods added in 2012)." it shows the distribution of chemicals across various basins, with the willamette river having the highest number of unique chemicals detected. the chart is color-coded to represent different categories of chemicals, such as consumer product constituents, current-use pesticides, industrial chemicals or intermediates, and metals. the bars are labeled with the names of the basins, and the y-axis indicates the number of chemicals detected. the chart also includes a note at the bottom stating, "figure 8: number of unique chemicals detected by chemical group per basin (excludes new methods added in 2012). see figure 4 for number of sites sampled in each basin listed in parentheses. sterois excluded from excluded from this figure since the same four sterols were detected across all basins."
